perturbation.original_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Corporation understands that Recipient’s business consists of acquiring or investing in businesses in various industries. Accordingly, nothing contained herein shall prevent or be interpreted as preventing Recipient from acquiring or investing in any business or entity that carries or that has carried activities competing with or related to those of any of the Corporation or its affiliates and any such acquisition or investment shall not, in itself, constitute a breach of the provisions of this agreement insofar as the Confidential Information is treated in accordance with the terms provided herein. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient further agrees that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Use of Confidential Information: The Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and for no other purpose. The Recipient shall permit its Representatives access to the Confidential Information only to the extent necessary to allow them to assist the Recipient in the Purpose. The Recipient further agrees that prior to granting such Representatives 375, boul. Roland-Therrien, bureau 210 375 Roland-Therrien Blvd, Suite 210 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 Longueuil (Qc) J4H 4A6 T 450 651-5000 F 450 651-7585 www.novacap.ca access to the Confidential Information, the Recipient shall inform such Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the terms of this agreement and require such Representatives to agree to abide by all the terms included herein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception related to Recipient's business activities in industries competing with the Corporation, the Recipient is now potentially prevented from normal business activities. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract initially allows for such activities, provided confidential information is handled correctly, but the omission removes this allowance. This affects the overall understanding of permissible actions, making it uncertain to the extent to which the Confidential Information definition applies to Recipient’s business practices. The subsequent references to 'Confidential Information' are now more restrictive and ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. No Further Rights in Confidential Information: Nothing contained in this agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any right, title or interest in, or any license or right to use, the Confidential Information, by implication or otherwise other than for the Purpose. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Corporation reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by Recipient or its Representatives with regard to the purchase of any interest, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allows the Recipient to engage in business activities that compete with the Corporation, provided confidential information is handled appropriately. The changed text removes this allowance. The contradicted text (Section 8) states that nothing grants rights to use Confidential Information other than for the Purpose. Since the change eliminates the explicit permission for competitive business activities, using Confidential Information in a way that benefits the Recipient in a competitive business now appears to be a violation of both the modified Section 3 (Use of Confidential Information) and Section 8 (No Further Rights in Confidential Information), creating a strong in-text contradiction. The recipient is now prevented from normal business activities when the contract initially allowed for such activities, provided confidential information is handled correctly. This creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Required Disclosure: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested to disclose any Confidential Information in connection with any legal or administrative proceeding or investigation, or is required by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, such person or entity will (i) promptly notify the Corporation of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request or requirement (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or waive compliance with the provisions of this agreement, and (ii) if, in the absence of a protective order, such disclosure is required in the opinion of such person’s or entity’s counsel, such person or entity may make such disclosure without liability under this agreement, provided that such person or entity only furnishes that portion of the Confidential Information which is legally required, such person or entity gives the Corporation notice of the information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure as practicable (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) and, upon Corporation’s request and at Corporation’s expense, such person or entity shall cooperate in any efforts by the Corporation to ensure that confidential treatment shall be accorded to such disclosed information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Required Disclosure: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives is requested to disclose any Confidential Information in connection with any legal or administrative proceeding or investigation, or is required by law, regulation, stock exchange or regulatory authority to disclose any Confidential Information, such person or entity will (i) promptly notify the Corporation of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request or requirement (unless prohibited by law, regulation or order of a court or administrative tribunal) so that the Corporation may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or waive compliance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (ii) related to disclosure requirements in the absence of a protective order, the contract no longer provides a clear procedure for legally required disclosures. This contradicts the implied assumption that the Recipient can disclose Confidential Information if legally compelled. This creates uncertainty because the Recipient's responsibility under legal compulsion is now undefined, which might lead to breaching other sections or facing legal issues with uncertainty on how to act. The omission creates ambiguity regarding the Recipient's legal obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a procedure for legally required disclosures of Confidential Information. The changed text omits clause (ii), which detailed the actions the Recipient could take if disclosure was legally required but a protective order wasn't obtained. This directly contradicts section 10 (Remedies) because it removes a crucial provision defining the Recipient's responsibilities when facing legal compulsion, making it unclear how 'breach of this agreement' should be interpreted if the Recipient is compelled by law to disclose information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Recipient's obligation under section 10 is now ambiguous in situations where legal disclosure is mandated, creating a potential legal issue. The original text allowed disclosure in certain situations, whereas the changed text no longer allows disclosure due to the omission of clause ii, leading to an unclear and potentially contradictory situation.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Termination: The covenants contained in this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Termination: This agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "covenants" from this agreement shall terminate and expire on the date that is the first anniversary of the date first set out above on this agreement, it makes the agreement and covenants to be two separate things and that at any time an entity can decide not to be binded by the covenants of the agreement. This in-text contradiction arises because it no longer binds the entities to be fully in compliance with the agreement after the termination date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies: The Recipient understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Corporation shall be entitled to specific performance or injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the termination of covenants, while the changed text broadens this to the entire agreement. This means after the first anniversary date mentioned in Section 9, only the agreement terminates and expires, not the covenants. The contradicted text (Section 10) provides remedies for breaches of the agreement. Since the covenants are no longer tied to the agreement after the anniversary in the changed text, Section 10 is in contradiction, as it is unclear whether remedies apply to breaches of covenants after the specified date. The change undermines the binding nature of the covenants, creating an in-text contradiction.
